title: Submodules, Nested Repositories, and Dependency Boundaries
description: A product pins three component repositories, one URL moved, a fork rewrites a dependency, recursive checkout executes untrusted configuration, and release builds disagree on nested state. Learners cumulatively produce submodule dependency and trust manifest through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Explain gitlink entries, superproject commits, submodule repositories, checked-out commits, .gitmodules, local config, and detached submodule HEAD.
Predict init, update, sync, set-url, set-branch, recursive, remote, merge, rebase, and shallow behavior across configuration layers.
Stage gitlink changes, publish dependency commits before superproject refs, review nested diffs, and verify that every referenced object is reachable.
